title: Tieghemopanax macgillivrayi
taxonomy: Tieghemopanax macgillivrayi is classified in the family Araliaceae. It belongs to the order Apiales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification of Tieghemopanax macgillivrayi includes the division Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae. This classification places Tieghemopanax macgillivrayi within the larger group of flowering plants. The species is also known by its synonym Panax macgillivrayi.

intro: Tieghemopanax macgillivrayi, also known as MacGillivray's sassafras, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions including arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tieghemopanax macgillivrayi include lignans, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In traditional New Zealand medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
